title: Chewy Fermented Chinese Burrata Congee
description: A savory, date-night dish from Yunnan Province featuring creamy burrata, miso-marinated meat, and a chewy fermented rice congee.
instructions: Combine glutinous rice and 4 cups water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and chewy.
Meanwhile, marinate the pork belly in a mixture of miso paste, Korean chili flakes, and ginger for at least 30 minutes.
Grill or pan-fry the pork belly until crispy and golden brown.
Cut the pork belly into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
Heat rice vinegar in a small saucepan over low heat until warm.
Stir the warm vinegar into the cooked glutinous rice and let cool to room temperature.
Once the rice has cooled, stir in the burrata cheese and chopped scallions.
To assemble the congee, place a scoop of the rice mixture into a bowl and top with a piece of pork belly, a sprinkle of scallions, and a drizzle of the miso marinade.
